title: Fabric CI/CD Lifecycle Best Practices
summary: End-to-end CI/CD lifecycle for Microsoft Fabric solutions: Git integration, feature workspaces, variable libraries, deployment strategies, auto-binding, data orchestration, and release processes. Covers single-workspace and multi-workspace solutions.

principles: Design the Fabric solution as a composition of workspace item types
Use a medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold) with lakehouses as storage containers
Determine if the solution fits in a single workspace or requires multiple workspaces
Multi-workspace: use for security isolation (staging vs presentation) or >1000 items
Use workspace folders to organize items by role or type within a workspace
multi_workspace_triggers: Security: business users should not see staging/ETL data
Ownership: different teams own different layers

environment_planning.capacity_isolation: Recommended: create a separate Fabric capacity per environment to isolate workload impact

git_integration.source_of_truth: The integration branch (e.g., 'main' or 'dev') is the single source of truth — always ready for deployment
git_integration.git_folder_setting: Use --directory with 'fabio git connect' to separate item definitions from workflow files (e.g., '/workspace')
git_integration.multi_workspace_git: Connect all workspaces to the SAME branch with different --directory values (e.g., '/workspace/staging', '/workspace/presentation')
git_integration.branch_policy: Configure the integration branch to only accept changes via pull requests — no direct commits

feature_workspaces.concept: Each developer works in an isolated feature workspace connected to a feature branch
workflow: 1. Create feature branch from integration branch
2. Create a new workspace (or reuse an existing feature workspace)
3. Connect workspace to the feature branch: fabio git connect --workspace <FW> --branch feature/xyz
4. Initialize (pulls items from branch): fabio git init --workspace <FW> --strategy prefer-remote
5. Work on changes in the feature workspace
6. Commit changes: fabio git commit --workspace <FW> --message 'Add feature XYZ' --commit-all
7. Create pull request to merge into integration branch
8. After merge, delete feature branch (keep branches short-lived)

lifecycle_patterns.create_and_delete: Highest isolation — one workspace per PR. More provisioning overhead.
lifecycle_patterns.recycle: Reuse feature workspaces across branches for dedicated teams. Lower overhead, but keep synchronized.

parameterization_strategy.preferred: Variable libraries with value sets (Microsoft's strategic recommendation)
parameterization_strategy.fallback: Deploy parameter files (find_replace, key_value_replace) for item types that don't support variable libraries yet
design_steps: 1. Identify all environment-specific settings (connection strings, paths, IDs)
2. Create a variable library with variables for each setting
3. Update items to read from the variable library at runtime
4. Add value sets for each environment (test, prod)
5. Name value sets to match --env values for auto-activation

auto_binding.concept: Auto-binding automatically reconnects item dependencies within the same workspace after deployment or Git sync
auto_binding.supported_via_logicalId: Items use logicalId (not workspace-specific IDs) in definitions, enabling Fabric to resolve dependencies in any workspace
auto_binding.notebook_requirement: Notebooks require notebook-settings.json in their item definition to enable auto-binding (added March 2026). Without it, lakehouse bindings won't auto-resolve.
auto_binding.cross_workspace: Auto-binding only works within a single workspace. For cross-workspace dependencies, use Item reference variables in a variable library.
auto_binding.post_sync_scripts: For item types that don't support auto-binding, write post-sync automation to reestablish relationships after deployment.

principles: Implement ETL logic using workspace items (notebooks, pipelines) — not external scripts
Expose a single top-level pipeline/notebook that runs end-to-end ETL
After deployment, run the top-level item to populate lakehouses with data
Use .schedules files in item definitions for ongoing data refresh
Manage schema changes via notebook code (add/remove tables and columns)
data_orchestration.post_deploy_pattern: fabio deploy apply --source ./workspace --workspace <WS> --env prod && fabio data-pipeline run --workspace <WS> --id <pipeline-id> --wait

deployment_pipeline.best_for: Low-code, small-to-medium projects focused on semantic models and reports

git_synchronization.best_for: Branch-based releases where target workspaces stay connected to Git
limitations: Shows Git status in production workspace

api_driven_fabio.best_for: Scalable releases with custom branching, parameterization, many workspaces
advantages: One-to-many: single branch → multiple target workspaces
Works cross-tenant with different service principals
api_driven_fabio.workflow: fabio deploy plan → review → fabio deploy apply --env <target>

security_best_practices: Use service principal authentication for all CI/CD automation
Never commit secrets to Git — use GitHub Secrets, Azure Key Vault, or $ENV: variables
Use OIDC federated credentials (recommended) for GitHub Actions instead of client secrets
Store Terraform state in encrypted cloud storage, not locally
Use Fabric connections (managed via Terraform) to hold data source credentials

full_lifecycle: 1. Infrastructure: Terraform provisions capacities, workspaces, connections, role assignments
2. Source of truth: fabio deploy export --workspace dev-ws --dir ./workspace (one-time)
3. Development: feature branches + feature workspaces + PRs to integration branch
4. Validation: fabio deploy validate --source ./workspace --parameters params.json --env prod
5. Plan: fabio deploy plan --source ./workspace --workspace prod-ws --env prod --parameters params.json
6. Apply: fabio deploy apply --source ./workspace --workspace prod-ws --env prod --parameters params.json
7. Post-deploy: Variable library value sets auto-activated, semantic models refreshed, environments published
8. Data orchestration: Run top-level pipeline to populate lakehouses
